title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Concepcion Rodriguez, 39, received a life sentence for a 2022 Fitchburg home invasion, with additional time for eight charges.
Rodriguez was convicted of crimes including armed assault, possession of a firearm, and theft; one firearm charge dismissed.
Rodriguez's attorney argues the habitual criminal sentencing violated rights and plans to appeal the convictions.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White feels increasingly like a Bostonian, embracing the city's culture and sports enthusiasm.
White celebrated Samuel Adams' new beer line, featuring beverages honoring Boston sports figures.
The Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference with a 53-25 record, surpassing expectations this NBA season.

title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hires former Nuggets coach Michael Malone as men's basketball coach, replacing Hubert Davis.
Mike Krzyzewski comments on UNC hiring Malone and Belichick, noting adjustment challenges from pro to college.
Bill Belichick's first season as UNC football coach ended with a 4-8 record, after replacing Mack Brown.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A dog was stabbed by a person in Milton during an alleged attack, State Police reported.
The incident occurred Monday morning on Blue Hill River Road, near a park and recreation area.
Milton Animal Control is investigating the incident, and the dog was taken to a local vet.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox's Trevor Story moves from No. 2 to No. 5 in batting order due to a .119 average.
Masataka Yoshida to bat second; Roman Anthony remains leadoff hitter with Jarren Duran benched.
Boston averages 3.3 runs per game, ranks 26th in MLB walk percentage and 5th in strikeouts.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers are currently fourth in the playoff seeding, one game behind the New York Knicks.
ESPN Cleveland's Andrew Goldhammer claims the Cavaliers want to avoid facing the Celtics in the second round.
The Celtics recently swept the regular-season series against Cleveland and have won 10 of their last 12 games.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents face the highest electricity costs in the continental U.S., with rates varying by location.
Locally-owned municipal utilities often offer half the cost of electricity compared to large investor-owned companies.
Switching to municipal utilities is costly, but municipal aggregation allows communities to purchase power in bulk.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students and faculty protested administrators' lack of communication after recent violence near campus.
The protest follows a fatal police shooting and other violent incidents near Northeastern, raising safety concerns.
Protesters demanded transparent updates as students rely on external reports for safety information rather than university alerts.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University President Melissa Gilliam paused a signage policy after Pride flag removals led to protests and backlash.
The contested policy prevents affixing materials, like Pride flags, to university property if outward-facing, sparking controversy.
The pause aims to allow further discussion and reflection on policy impacts, particularly concerning the LGBTQ+ community.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Red Sox completed the Sonny Gray trade by sending pitching prospect Patrick Galle to the Cardinals.
Boston traded Brandon Clarke, Richard Fitts, and Galle for Gray and cash from St. Louis in November.
Patrick Galle, drafted from Ole Miss, started this season with Single A Salem and is primarily a relief pitcher.
